seoTitle: How to Draw Lines Using Canva Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to draw lines in Canva to guide the eye, divide layouts, and add structure to social graphics, presentations, and branding materials.
intro: Lines are a simple but effective design tool in Canva — they can separate sections, underline headings, or add visual structure to a busy layout. This guide shows how to add and customize line elements so your designs feel more organized and intentional.

steps.heading: Open the Elements panel
steps.body: Click Elements in the left sidebar and search 'lines' to see the available line and shape styles.

steps.heading: Drag a line onto your canvas
steps.body: Choose a straight, dashed, or decorative line and drag it into your design.

steps.heading: Resize and rotate the line
steps.body: Click and drag the endpoints to change its length or angle so it fits your layout.

steps.heading: Customize color and thickness
steps.body: Use the toolbar to change the line's color and weight to match your design's style.

steps.heading: Position it relative to other elements
steps.body: Move the line so it visually connects or separates text and images the way you intend.

faq.question: Can I curve a line in Canva instead of keeping it straight?
faq.answer: Yes, some line elements include curve handles, or you can search for curved-line graphics in Elements.

faq.question: Are line elements free to use in Canva?
faq.answer: Most basic lines are free; some decorative or animated line styles may require Canva Pro.

faq.question: Can I use the drawing tool instead of a line element?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva's draw tool lets you sketch a freehand line if you want something less rigid than a straight element.

faq.question: How do I make sure my line stays aligned?
faq.answer: Canva shows alignment guides automatically as you drag the line near other elements on the canvas.
